powers conferred by section 43, read with section 75, of the Disaster Management Act, 2005 (53 of 2005), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely: - PART-1 Preliminary 1. Short title and commencement.- (1) These rules may be called the National Institute of Disaster Management Employees (Recruitment and Other Conditions of Service) Rules, 2014. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Definition.- In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires,- (a) “Act” means the Disaster Management Act, 2005 (53 of 2005); -- 31 of 62 -- 32 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (b) “academic staff” means the persons appointed to the faculty posts of (Research Associate, Assistant Professors, Associate Professors, Professors) and Consultants; (c) “appointing authority” in relation to any post in the Institute, means the authority competent to make appointment to that post. (d) “borrowed employee” means an employee of any authority whose services are obtained by the Institute on loan; (e) “employee” means a person serving in the Institute in any sanctioned post of the Institute; (f) “foreign service” means service for which an employee received with the approval of the appointing authority, his pay from any source other than the funds of the Institute; (g) “Institute” means the National Institute of Disaster Management constituted vide notification of the Government of India in the Ministry of Home Affairs number S.O. 1862€, dated the 30th October, 2006 ; (h) “pay” means the pay admissible on the relevant date and includes special pay and personal pay as well as deputation allowance in case of borrowed employees but does not include any other allowances, fee or honorarium; (i) “permanent post” means a post carrying a definite rate of pay sanctioned without limit to time; (j) “regular service” in relation to any grade means the appointment to a sanctioned post according to prescribed procedure and after completion of probation; (k) “sanctioning authority” means - (i) in relation to Group A posts; the Central Government; (ii) in relation to Group B and C posts; the Executive Director; (l) “schedule” means a schedule to these rules; (m) “temporary employee” means the service of a temporary employee in a temporary post or officiating service in a permanent post; (n) “temporary post” means a post carrying a definite rate of pay for a limited time; (o) “governing body” means the Governing Body of the Institute constituted under sub-section(4) of Section 42 of the Disaster Management Act, 2005. PART- II TERMS AND CONDITIONS FOR RECRUITMENT 3. Name, number of posts, classification, etc..–The name, number, classification, pay band and grade pay of various categories of posts in the Institute shall be as specified in columns (1) to (4) of the schedule. 4. Method of recruitment, age-limit and other qualifications etc. .-The method of recruitment, age- limit, qualifications and other matters connected thereto shall be as specified in columns (5) to (13) of the Schedule. 5. Initial Constitution.- (1) All employees appointed to various posts in regular pay band and grade pay shall be deemed to have been appointed under these rules to the respective posts with effect from the date of their service in the Institute. -- 32 of 62 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 33 (2) The services rendered in the Institute by the employees referred to in sub-rule (1) shall be counted for the purpose of qualifying service for promotion, confirmation, period of probation and pension from the date of their service in the Institute. PART- III APPOINTMENT, RETIREMENT AND RESIGNATION 6. Seniority.-The seniority of employee in each category shall be determined by the order of merit in which they were selected for appointment to the grade, and shall be governed by their relative seniority obtained on the date of their initial appointment in the Institute. 7. Probation.- (1) Every employee appointed to a post in the Institute by direct recruitment shall be on probation for such period as specified in column (9) of the Schedule : Provided that the Executive Director may, for reasons to be recorded in writing and with the prior approval of the Central Government, extend the period of probation in such cases as may be considered necessary : Provided further that any decision for extension of a probation period shall be taken within eight weeks after the expiry of the probationary period and communicated in writing to the concerned employee together with the reasons for so doing. (2) The extended period of probation referred to in sub-rule (1), should not exceed half of the specified probation period in any case. (3) On completion of the period of probation or any extension thereof, employees shall, if considered fit for appointment, be retained in their appointments on regular basis and confirmed within eight weeks of the expiry of such probation. (4) If, during the period of probation or any extension thereof, as the case may be, the Institute is of the opinion that an officer is not fit for permanent appointment, the Institute may discharge or revert the candidate to the post held by him prior to this appointment, as the case may be, or pass such orders as deem fit. 8. All appointments to various posts shall be made by the Institute with the prior approval of the Central Government. 9. Certificate of fitness and antecedents.- No person shall be appointed to any post by direct recruitment unless (a) he produces at his own cost a certificate of physical fitness in the prescribed format from a medical practitioner so authorised by the Institute: Provided that no such certificate shall be required in case of temporary appointments for a period of less than six months: Provided further that any extension beyond six months shall require a medical certificate of fitness. (b) the appointing authority is satisfied that he or she possesses good character and antecedents. 10. Liability for service in any part of India.- Employees of the Institute shall be liable to serve anywhere in India. 11. Deputation.- Any employee, who is appointed on deputation basis will be governed by the service rules of his parent cadre for the purposes of discipline, conduct, provident fund, pensionary benefits, leave, leave travel concession, group insurance scheme and related matters. 12. Disqualifications.- No person – (a) who has entered into or contracted a marriage with a person having a spouse living, or (b) who, having a spouse living has entered into or contracted a marriage with any person, shall be eligible for appointment in the Institute : -- 33 of 62 -- 34 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] Provided that the Central Government may, if satisfied that such marriage is permissible under the personal law applicable to such person and the other party to the marriage and that there are other grounds for so doing, exempt any person from the operation of this rule. 13. Power to relax.- Where the Central Government is of the opinion that it is necessary or expedient so to do, it may, by orders and for reasons to be recorded in writing, relax any of the provisions of these rules with respect to any class or category of persons. 14. Saving.- Nothing in these rules shall affect reservations, relaxation of age limit and other concessions required to be provided for persons belonging to the Scheduled Caste and the Scheduled Tribes and other backward classes, the ex-servicemen and other special categories of persons in accordance with the orders issued by the Central Government from time to time in this regard. 15. Termination of Service.- (1) The services of a temporary employee may be terminated by the appointing authority without assigning reasons- (a) during the period of probation following the first appointment, and after such period of probation, and before confirmation at any time, by notice of one month in writing or on payment of pay for such period as the notice falls short of one month or at any time without notice on payment of one month’s pay; (b) in the case of temporary employee appointed for work in any project being implemented by the Institute including research projects, the manner of termination and the period of notice shall be as specified in the order of appointment. (2) Without prejudice to the provisions of clause (a) of the sub-rule (1), the services of a temporary employee shall terminate- (a) if his appointment is made for a specific period, on the expiry of such period; or (b) if his appointment is made against a temporary post, on the abolition of the post or on the expiry of the period for which the post is created. (3) An employee who is given notice of termination of service under clause (a) of sub-rule (1) may be granted during the period of notice, at the discretion of the appointing authority such earned leave as may be admissible to him. 16. Lien.-A permanent employee of the Institute shall hold lien generally for not more than two years on the post substantively held by him provided his appointment in the other organisation was made through proper channel. 17. Retirement.-An employee shall retire from the institute - (a) on his attaining the age of sixty years; or (b) on his being declared medically unfit for service by a Medical Board to be designated by the Institute in this behalf. 18. Voluntary retirement.- (1) Employees of the Institute, who have put in not less than twenty years’ qualifying service may, by giving notice of three months in writing to the appointing authority, retire from service voluntarily: Provided that a notice of less than three months may be accepted in deserving cases, with the concurrence of the appointing authority. (2) If an employee applies for voluntary retirement while he is on leave not due, without returning to duty, the retirement shall take effect from the date of commencement of the leave not due and the leave salary paid in respect of such leave not due shall be recovered. (3) Before an employee gives notice of voluntary retirement, he shall satisfy himself by means of a reference to the Administration that he has, in fact, completed twenty years’ qualifying service. -- 34 of 62 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 35 (4) A notice of voluntary retirement may be withdrawn subsequently only with the approval of the appointing authority provided the request for such withdrawal is made before the expiry of the notice. (5) A notice of voluntary retirement given after completion of twenty years’ qualifying service will require acceptance by the appointing authority if the date of retirement on the expiry of the notice would be earlier than the date on which the employee concerned could have retired under the existing rules applicable to him. (6) Acceptance may be generally given in all cases except– (a) where disciplinary proceedings are pending or contemplated against the employee and the disciplinary authority, having regard to the circumstances of the case, is of the view that the imposition of the penalty of removal or dismissal from service would be warranted in the case; or (b) where prosecution is contemplated or may have been launched in a Court of law against the employee concerned. (7) An employee giving notice of voluntary retirement may presume its acceptance by the appointing authority and the retirement shall be effective in terms of the notice unless the appointing authority issues an order to the contrary before the expiry of the period of notice. (8) Institute employees on deputation to other organisations shall not be eligible to apply for retirement during the period of such deputation. (9) An employee giving notice of voluntary retirement may also apply, before the expiry of the notice, for the leave standing to his credit which may be granted to him to run concurrently with the period of notice. 19. Resignation.- (1) A temporary employee may, by notice of one month in writing addressed to the appointing authority, resign from the service of the Institute. (2) A permanent employee may, by giving three months’ notice in writing addressed to the appointing authority, resign from the service of the Institute: Provided that the appointing authority may allow a notice of lesser period under certain special circumstances for resignation under this rule. PART- IV PAY 20. Initial pay.- (1) An employee shall, on his appointment to a post, draw pay in the pay band and grade pay attached to that post: Provided that the sanctioning authority may decide, subject to sub-rule (2), that employee shall draw pay at any higher stage in the pay band. (2) Fixation of pay band, grade pay or scale of pay of employees, appointed or promoted to the new posts involving the assumption of duties or responsibilities of greater importance or otherwise or transferred to another post on account of inefficiency or misbehaviour or on request shall be regulated on the analogy of the rules applicable to the Central Government employees from time to time. 21. Increments.- An increment shall be drawn in the pay band of pay on 1st July of the year provided the employee has completed six months of service in that grade @ 3% of the sum of pay in pay band and grade pay applicable rounded off to next multiple of 10, unless it is withheld under the provision of these rules. 22. Service for increments.-The following service shall count for increments in the pay band of a post, namely - -- 35 of 62 -- 36 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (a) duty in that post or in any other post in the same or higher grade, whether continuous or not; (b) duty in an equivalent or higher post in foreign service; and (c) leave, other than extraordinary leave: Provided that the sanctioning authority may direct that extraordinary leave shall also count for increments if it is satisfied that such leave was taken on account of illness or for any other cause beyond the control of the employee. 23. Pay during leave.- (1) An employee on casual leave or compensatory leave or quarantine leave shall draw pay as on duty. (2) An employee on earned leave, commuted leave, shall draw pay as drawn by him before proceeding on leave. (3) An employee on leave on half pay shall draw pay at half the rate of pay drawn by him before proceeding on leave. (4) An employee on study leave shall draw pay at such rate as may be specified by the Government of India. (5) No pay shall be admissible to an employee on extraordinary leave. (6) An employee shall draw leave salary equal to the pay drawn by him immediately before proceeding on earned leave. 24. Pay during suspension.-An employee under suspension shall, during the period of suspension, be paid subsistence allowance as admissible to an employee in the Government of India. 25. Special pay,personal pay, honorarium and fee.-The Institute may sanction to an employee, in any special circumstances, such special pay, personal pay, honorarium or fee on such conditions as may be determined by the sanctioning authority. 26. Drawal of pay.- (1) An employee shall be entitled to the pay of the post to which he is appointed from the date on which he assumes charge of the post. (2) The pay and allowances to the employees of the Institute shall be payable on the last working day of the month to which they relate, except for the month of March, which shall, however, continue to be payable on the first working day of April. (3) An employee resigning from the service of the Institute without the notice required under rule 21 shall not, unless the appointing authority directs otherwise, be allowed to draw pay due but not drawn. Provided that the pay so not allowed to be drawn shall not exceed the pay for one month. 27. Bonus.-Payment of adhoc bonus to the employees of the Institute shall be at the same rate and subject to the same limits as applicable to the employees of the Central Government from time to time. PART- V ALLOWANCES 28. Allowances.–All allowances as admissible to the Central Government employees from time to time shall be admissible to the employees of the Institute at the same rates and on the same terms and conditions as applicable to the Central Government employees. 29. Suspension and subsistence grant.-Rules relating to suspension and subsistence grant thereof as applicable to Central Government employees shall also apply to the employees of the Institute. 30. Leave travel concession.-The employees of the Institute shall be entitled to leave travel concessions on the same terms and conditions as are admissible to the employees of the Central Government of equivalent grade from time to time. -- 36 of 62 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 37 31. Drawal of allowance.- (1) Dearness allowance, Transport allowance and house rent allowance admissible to an employee for any month shall be payable along with the pay of the employee. (2) Journey allowance, leave travel concession and daily allowance shall be settled in respect of the employees of the Institute subject to the provision of adjustment of advance, if any, drawn from the Institute. (3) An employee resigning from the Institute without the notice shall not, unless the appointing authority directs otherwise, be allowed to draw the allowance due but not drawn: Provided that the allowances not allowed to be drawn shall not exceed one months allowances. 32. Deputation and training allowance.- (1) The employees of the Institute on deputation basis shall be given an option to either opt for the pay scale and other service benefits of the Institute or to retain their own pay scales and to get deputation allowance/training allowance as per the existing instructions of the Government of India on the subject. (2) Executive Director of the Institute shall draw sumptuary allowance at the rates admissible to Heads of Training Institutions of the Central Government. PART- VI FACILITIES 33. Outdoor medical facilities.- (1) Employees of the Institute shall be eligible to get medical reimbursement for self and declared members of family. Explanation.-For the purposes of this sub-rule, the expression “family” shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in the Central Services (Medical Attendance) Rules, 1944. (2) The reimbursement of outdoor medical expenses during the year shall be limited to the actual expenses or one month’s pay as on 1st January of the year (Basic Pay + Dearness Allowance), whichever is less. (3) The claim should be supported by Doctor’s prescription and the original cash memos or bills for treatment by the Doctor and purchase of medicines. (4) For employees of the Institute joining during the year, the annual entitlement shall be restricted on pro-rata basis. (5) The outdoor treatment shall be taken from the Authorised Medical Attendants from the panel to be maintained by the Institute. Indoor treatment.- (1) For the purpose of indoor treatment the employees of the Institute shall be entitled for medical treatment at hospitals authorised by the Institute in this behalf, and for this purpose cost of treatment including hospital accommodation, nursing home facility, etc. shall be as per the provisions of the Central Services (Medical Attendants) Rules, 1944 as applicable to the Central Government employees drawing equivalent pay. (2) The authorised hospitals for the purpose of sub-rule (1) above shall be the same as are available to the Central Government employees regulated by Central Services (Medical Attendance) Rules, 1944. (3) Treatment at authorised hospitals may be taken on the advice of the Authorised Medical Attendants except in emergency. 34. Advances.-The Government of India rules with regard to advances such as festival advance, motor car advance, personal computer advance, house building advance, fan advance etc., shall be applicable to the employees of the Institute as per the rates and subject to the same limits as applicable to the employees of the Central Government from time to time. -- 37 of 62 -- 38 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] 35. Group insurance.- (1) Employees of the Institute other than those on deputation, shall be entitled to Group Insurance in consultation with the Central Government. (2) Employees of the Institute appointed on deputation shall continue to be governed by the Group Insurance Schemes as are applicable to them in their parent Ministry or Department or Organisation. (3) The Institute shall recover contribution towards the Insurance schemes from the employees referred in sub-rule (2) and remit the amount immediately to the lending Ministry or Department or Organisation and any loss of interest on account of late remittance thereof shall be borne by the Institute. 36. Conveyance allowance.-The employees of the Institute shall be allowed the option to make use of an official vehicle or reimbursement of such amount as is admissible to the Central Government officers in equivalent pay and grade, for official work. 37. Telephone facility.-The Employees of the Institute shall be eligible for telephone facilities as admissible to officers of the Central Government drawing equivalent pay. 38. Expenses for official meetings.-The Employees of the Institute shall be eligible for expenses for official meetings as admissible to officers of the Central Government drawing equivalent pay. PART VII LEAVE 39. Leave rules.–The Central Civil Services (Leave) Rules, 1972, as applicable to Central Government employees shall also apply to the employees of the Institute. 40. Procedure for grant of leave.- (1) An employee shall before proceeding on leave, make an application in the form specified by the Institute and shall also state in writing his address and telephone number while on leave and shall keep the Institute informed of any subsequent change in such address. (2) The Executive Director shall be the competent authority to dispose of all applications for leave other than study leave. (3) In the case of Executive Director, the Central Government shall be the competent authority to sanction leave including study leave. (4) The Institute shall maintain a leave account in respect of every employee. PART VIII PENSION AND OTHER BENEFITS 41. (1) Pension.-The provisions of the New Pension Scheme, 2004 along with the various orders and directions issued thereunder by the Government of India shall be applicable to all the employees of the Institute. (2) Career advancement.- The provisions of career advancement as applicable to academic staff under the University Grants Commission shall be applicable to the academic staff of the Institute. (3) Modified Assured Career Progression.-The provisions of Modified Assured Career Progression Scheme as applicable to Central Government employees shall be applicable to the non-academic staff of the Institute. -- 38 of 62 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 39 PART IX CONDUCT, DISCIPLINE AND PENALTIES 42. Conduct.- (1) The Central Civil Services (Conduct) Rules, 1964 shall also apply to the employees of the Institute. (2) The Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965, shall also apply to the employees of the Institute. (3) Except where specifically provided otherwise, the Governing Body of the Institute, shall exercise the same powers in respect of its employees as are exercisable by the President of the Union of India in respect of Central Government employees. (4) The appointing authority shall be the disciplinary authority and the powers of the appellate authority shall be exercised by the next higher authority. (5) In respect of the Central Government or State Government servant employees borrowed by the Institute, the provisions respectively of rules 20 and 21 of the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal) Rules, 1965 shall apply. 43. Suspension.- (1) The appointing authority or any other authority superior to that authority may place an employee under suspension - (a) where a disciplinary proceeding against him in respect of serious charge likely to result in a major penalty being inflicted, is contemplated or is pending; or (b) where a case against him in respect of any criminal offence is under investigation or trial. (2) An employee who is detained in custody, whether on a criminal charge or otherwise, for a period exceeding forty-eight hours shall be deemed to have been suspended with effect from the date of his detention, by an order of the Appointing Authority and shall remain under suspension until further orders. (3) An order of suspension made or deemed to have been made under this rule may at any time be revoked by the authority which made or is deemed to have made it or by any superior authority. 44. Special Provision regarding borrowed employees.- (1) Where an order of suspension is made or a disciplinary proceeding is commenced against a borrowed employee, the lending authority shall forthwith be informed of the circumstances leading to the order of suspension or commencement of the disciplinary proceedings, as the case may be. (2) In the light of the findings in the disciplinary proceeding taken against such employee- -- 39 of 62 -- 40 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (a) if the authority imposing the penalty is of the opinion that any of the major penalties should be imposed on him, it shall place his services at the disposal of the lending authority and transmit to it the proceedings of the inquiry for such action as it deems necessary; and (b) if the authority imposing the penalty is of the opinion that any other penalty should be imposed on him, it may, after consultation with the lending authority, pass such orders on the case as it deems necessary: Provided that in the event of a difference of opinion between the lending authority and the authority imposing the penalty, the service of the employee shall be placed at the disposal of the lending authority. Explanation.–For the purposes of these rules, "lending authority" means the authority which has placed the services of the borrowed employee at the disposal of the Institute. PART X APPEALS AND REVIEW 46. Appellate authorities.- An appeal shall lie from any original order made by the Executive Director under these rules to the Central Government, and from an order made by an authority lower in rank than the Executive Director, to the Executive Director. 47. Period of limitation for appeals.-No appeal shall be entertained unless it is submitted within a period of forty-five days from the date on which the order appealed against is communicated to the person concerned: Provided that the appellate authority may entertain the appeal after the expiry of the said period if it is satisfied that the appellant had sufficient cause for not submitting the appeal in time. 48. Form contents and submission of appeals.– (1) Every person submitting an appeal shall do so separately and in his own name. (2) The appeal shall be addressed to the appellate authority which shall contain all material statements and arguments on which the appellant relies, shall not contain any disrespectful or improper language and shall be complete in itself. (3) Every appeal shall be submitted to the Executive Director who shall, unless he is himself the appellate authority, transmit it to the appellate authority. 49. Consideration of appeals.-The appellate authority shall consider every appeal and pass such orders as it deems proper in the circumstances of the case: Provided that no order imposing an enhanced penalty shall be passed unless the appellant is given an opportunity of making any representation which he may wish to make against such enhanced penalty. 50. Review.-For the purposes of review, the procedure specified in the Central Civil Services (Classification, Control and Appeal (CCA) Rules, 1965 shall also apply to the employees of the Institute. 51. Orders on re-instatement.-Where an employee who has been dismissed or suspended is reinstated, the authority reinstating him shall make an order specifying - (a) whether the employee may draw for the period of his absence from duty, any pay and allowances in addition to the pay and allowances admissible; (b) whether such period may be treated as a period spent on duty for all or any purposes. PART XI MISCELLANEOUS 52. Residential accommodation for employees.- (1) The employees of the Institute may be provided with unfurnished accommodation in its own campus after completion of construction of such buildings. -- 40 of 62 -- ¹Hkkx IIμ[k.M 3 (i)º Hkkjr dk jkti=k % vlk/kj.k 41 (2) The employees of the Institute may be provided with unfurnished accommodation taken on lease by the Institute till such time its own campus is developed and buildings are constructed: Provided that the licence fee for such accommodation under this rule shall be charged in accordance with the provision of F.R. 45 A as amended from time to time. (3) Employees of the Institute including those taken on deputation shall be entitled for house rent allowance as admissible to the employees of the Central Government drawing similar pay. 53. Holidays.–The Institute shall observe such holidays as may be determined by the Central Government for offices situated in Delhi. 54. Service books and character rolls.– (1) The Institute shall maintain a service book and character roll of each employee in such form and setting out such particulars as may be specified by the Governing Body. (2) The entries in the service book of an employee shall be made by the authorised officer of the Institute. (3) The entries in the character roll of an employee shall be made according to the procedure laid down by the appointing authority. 55. Delegation of power by Executive Director.–The Executive Director may subject to the approval of the Governing Body, delegate any of the powers vested in him under these rules, to any of the officers of the Institute. 56. Residuary conditions of service.–Any matter relating to the conditions of service of an employee for which no provision is made in these rules shall be determined by the Institute. 57.
